studies.tag: Enterprise Tool Introduction & Adoption · Government & Public Sector
studies.title: Enterprise Platform Rollout Across Multiple Business Units
studies.context: A large public sector organisation needed to introduce a new enterprise practice management platform across multiple business teams, managing readiness, training, integration, and operational adoption simultaneously — with significant process change and high user expectations.
studies.challenge: Platform implementation was technically on track, but business readiness, stakeholder alignment, and adoption planning had not kept pace. The risk of a low-adoption go-live was escalating as the delivery date approached.
studies.role: Infocus Group was engaged to provide business readiness leadership, stakeholder engagement, implementation planning coordination, integration oversight, training design, communications, and adoption tracking from mobilisation through to post-go-live sustainment.
outcomes: Improved go-live readiness across all business teams
Structured adoption plan with clear ownership
Better alignment between business and technology teams
Operating model and support structure established before cutover

studies.title: Data Foundations Redesign for Reporting and Analytics Confidence
studies.context: A regulated financial services organisation needed stronger data foundations across reporting, analytics, data warehouse, and enterprise data services to support executive decision-making and regulatory obligations.
studies.challenge: Multiple legacy systems, inconsistent data quality, unclear ownership, and a fragmented reporting environment were creating significant risk and limiting the organisation's ability to trust or act on its own data.
studies.role: Current-state assessment, target-state architecture design, data governance framework, delivery roadmap, stakeholder alignment, and technical delivery support across the data warehouse and analytics environments.
outcomes: Clearer data architecture with defined ownership across domains
Improved reporting confidence at executive level
Stronger data governance model and stewardship structure
Better analytics enablement for key business units
Foundations prepared for AI and ML workloads

studies.title: Secure Platform Connectivity Across Identity and Workflow Systems
studies.context: A large enterprise needed to connect multiple platforms securely and reliably — including identity and access management, workflow systems, and data integrations — across a complex multi-vendor environment.
studies.challenge: Point-to-point integrations had created brittle, hard-to-maintain connections. SSO was inconsistently implemented across platforms. A new system introduction required all integration touchpoints to be rationalised and validated before go-live.
studies.role: Integration planning, API coordination, SSO architecture and readiness, testing strategy design, defect tracking, and go-live support across multiple integration workstreams in parallel.
outcomes: Improved system interoperability across all key platforms
More secure and consistent access experience for end users
Reduced manual workarounds and reconciliation overhead
Stronger integration testing framework retained post-engagement
Smoother transition to live operations with fewer critical defects
